chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the framework repository: [https://github.com/runphi](https://github.com/runphi). This meets the criteria for 2 points.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation uses both emulated environments (e.g., QEMU mentioned in Section IV-A) and real hardware deployment, specifically mentioning the Xilinx Kria KV260 [29]. This qualifies as using physical testbeds/real hardware, warranting 2 points.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: The paper has a clear, explicit discussion of fault isolation (Property 3 - Failure isolation) and temporal isolation. It defines the system's reliance on partitioning hypervisors to achieve freedom from interference, explicitly stating that ZICs survive the crash of the privileged VM hosting the agent. This constitutes a formal definition of the required resilience/fault model, warranting 2 points.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs. It compares boot times (Fig. 4), measures temporal isolation under co-located stress using activation and response latency (Fig. 7), and discusses overheads like storage size for images and computational overhead compared to Linux containers in Section IV-B. This warrants 2 points.

major_weakness: The paper is exceptionally strong across all metrics; the only minor weakness noted is that network guarantees for ZICs are described as 'limited real-time' in Section IV-B.
